{"step_by_step_thinking": "The net filtration pressure (NFP) is calculated using the formula: NFP = (PGC - PBC) - (pGC - PBC). Given the data: PGC = 42 mm Hg, pGC = 12 mm Hg, and PBC = 16 mm Hg, we can substitute these values into the formula. NFP = (42 - 16) - (12 - 16) = 26 - (-4) = 26 + 4 = 30 mm Hg. Since none of the provided options match 30 mm Hg exactly, we need to choose the closest value. The closest option to 30 mm Hg is 28 mm Hg.", "answer_choice": "D"}
